headerDisplay: Bridges a Mitsubishi Electric M-NET centralized AC controller (G-50A / GB-50A) to Apple HomeKit. Each indoor unit appears as a HomeKit HeaterCooler accessory.
footerDisplay: See [project README](https://github.com/jozemlakar/homebridge-mnet#readme) for the protocol details and supported controllers.

name.title: Name
name.description: Displayed in the Homebridge logs. Not visible in HomeKit.

host.title: Controller IP or hostname
host.description: Reachable address of the G-50A / GB-50A controller.

port.title: HTTP port
port.description: Almost always 80. Change only if the controller sits behind a reverse proxy.

pollIntervalMs.title: Poll interval (ms)
pollIntervalMs.description: How often to read state from the controller. Lower = snappier HomeKit reflection, higher = lighter load.

autoModeDeadbandC.title: AUTO-mode deadband (°C)
autoModeDeadbandC.description: The controller has one SetTemp per unit; HomeKit's AUTO mode wants two thresholds. We bracket the controller's setpoint by ±half this value.

groupNames.title: Group display names
groupNames.description: Optional per-group display-name overrides. Without any entries here, accessories appear as "M-NET Group N".

group.title: Group number
group.description: Group as registered on the controller (1-50).

name.title: Display name
name.description: Name shown in HomeKit, e.g. "Living Room".

excludeGroups.title: Excluded groups
excludeGroups.description: Group numbers to hide from HomeKit (e.g. a test unit).
